title: Prog Enhancement and Graceful Degradation Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the two main strategies for building robust, cross-browser web experiences.

questions.question: Which philosophy starts with a basic, functional experience and then adds layers of sophistication for modern browsers?
Which layers of a website are considered the foundation of Progressive Enhancement (must work everywhere)?
In which philosophy do you first build for the most modern browsers and then ensure the experience still 'works' (but more simply) in older ones?
What is the name of the practice where you check if a specific API (like 'serviceWorker') exists before using it?
Why is 'Feature Detection' generally better than 'Browser Sniffing'?
options: It is more reliable because it checks the actual capability, not the version number of the browser (which can be faked).
It is easier to write in CSS.
